entry.title: Azure Security Center
entry.description: Azure Security Center provides unified security management and threat protection across Azure resources and hybrid cloud workloads.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers continuous assessment and security recommendations for Azure resources.
limits: Continuous security assessment for Azure resources
caveats: Advanced threat protection features require paid tier
Some integrations and hybrid features are not included

useCases: Monitor Azure resources for security issues
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Navigate to Security Center from the left menu.
Enable Security Center on your subscription.
Review security recommendations and apply suggested actions.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need basic security monitoring and recommendations for Azure resources without advanced threat protection.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced threat detection, multi-cloud support, or deep hybrid security features without paying for the paid tier.
entry.body: Azure Security Center's free tier provides basic security posture management and recommendations for Azure resources, making it suitable for monitoring and compliance needs without advanced protection.

entry.title: GitGuardian
entry.description: GitGuardian scans public and private repositories for secrets and sensitive data leaks to help prevent security breaches.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for public repositories and limited private repo scanning.
caveats: Private repo scanning is limited on free tier

useCases: Monitoring public repositories for sensitive data
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a GitGuardian account.
Connect your GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket repositories.
Configure scanning settings and select repositories to monitor.
Review detected incidents and alerts in the dashboard.
Set up notifications or integrations if needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to monitor repositories for accidental secret leaks or enforce security compliance in your codebase.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited private repo scanning for teams or need on-premises deployment.
entry.body: GitGuardian helps developers and security teams detect and remediate secret leaks in code repositories, supporting both public and private projects with automated scanning.

entry.title: Hostedscan.com
entry.description: Hostedscan.com provides automated vulnerability scanning for websites and servers with a free monthly scan allowance.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes 1 scan per month for 1 target.
caveats: No advanced scheduling or reporting on free tier

useCases: Periodic security monitoring for small projects
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Hostedscan.com account.
Add your website or server as a scan target.
Configure scan settings (choose scan type and options).
Start your first vulnerability scan.
Review the scan results and recommended actions.
entry.whenToUse: Use Hostedscan.com to quickly check your website or server for common vulnerabilities on a monthly basis.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need frequent scans, advanced reporting, or coverage for multiple assets without upgrading.
entry.body: Hostedscan.com offers a simple way to run automated vulnerability scans on your website or server, making it suitable for small projects or initial security checks. The free tier is limited to one scan per month for a single target, with no payment card required. This makes it a good fit for individuals or small teams needing basic, periodic security validation without advanced features or frequent scanning.

entry.title: IPTrace
entry.description: IPTrace provides free IP geolocation and lookup APIs for developers and businesses.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers a limited number of IP lookup API requests per month.

quickstartSteps: Visit https://iptrace.io and sign up for a free account.
Review the API documentation for endpoint details.
Copy your API key if required (for advanced usage).
Send a GET request to the IPTrace endpoint with the target IP address.
Parse the JSON response for geolocation data.
entry.whenToUse: Use IPTrace when you need quick, simple IP geolocation or lookup for small-scale projects or prototyping.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high request volumes, advanced data, or enterprise-grade SLAs.
entry.body: IPTrace offers a simple, free API for basic IP geolocation and lookup, ideal for low-volume, non-critical use cases and prototyping.

entry.title: Langtrace
entry.description: Langtrace provides observability and tracing for LLM applications to monitor, debug, and optimize prompt engineering workflows.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes unlimited traces and projects for individuals and small teams.
caveats: Advanced features may require paid plan

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Langtrace account at https://langtrace.ai.
Create a new project in the dashboard.
Install the Langtrace SDK in your application.
Instrument your LLM calls with the SDK.
View traces and debug sessions in the Langtrace dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Langtrace to monitor and debug LLM workflows or prompt chains during development and early production.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require enterprise-grade SLAs, advanced analytics, or integration with proprietary observability stacks.
entry.body: Langtrace offers free observability and tracing for LLM applications, helping developers monitor, debug, and optimize prompt workflows with minimal setup.

entry.title: Mozilla Observatory
entry.description: Mozilla Observatory is a free web security scanner that analyzes HTTP response headers and TLS configuration for best practices.

freeTierDetails.summary: Completely free online tool for unlimited web security scans.

useCases: Educate teams on web security best practices
quickstartSteps: Go to https://observatory.mozilla.org/
Enter your website URL in the input field.
Click 'Scan Me' to start the analysis.
Review the security report and recommendations.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a quick, free assessment of your website's HTTP headers and TLS security configuration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require private scans, automated API integration, or in-depth vulnerability testing beyond headers and TLS.
entry.body: Mozilla Observatory is a free online tool that helps users assess and improve the security of their web applications by analyzing HTTP headers and TLS configuration. It is ideal for quick checks, educational purposes, and benchmarking security improvements, but is not suitable for private or automated scans.

entry.title: OpenObserve.ai
entry.description: OpenObserve.ai is an open source observability platform for logs, metrics, and traces with a focus on cost efficiency.

freeTierDetails.summary: Self-hosted open source with no enforced usage limits.
limits: No enforced limits on logs, metrics, or traces
caveats: Resource usage depends on your own infrastructure

quickstartSteps: Install Docker and Docker Compose on your server.
Clone the OpenObserve repository from GitHub.
Run the provided Docker Compose file to start OpenObserve.
Access the web UI at http://localhost:5080 and create your first user.
Configure your applications to send logs or metrics to OpenObserve endpoints.
entry.whenToUse: Use OpenObserve.ai when you need a free, self-hosted observability solution for logs, metrics, and traces with minimal resource requirements.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require a fully managed SaaS solution or need advanced enterprise features and support.
entry.body: OpenObserve.ai provides a unified, open source platform for collecting and analyzing logs, metrics, and traces, designed for easy self-hosting and cost efficiency.

entry.title: PostHog
entry.description: PostHog is an open-source product analytics platform for tracking user behavior and feature usage.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes core analytics features for small projects and teams.
caveats: Event volume over limit requires upgrade

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free PostHog Cloud account or deploy self-hosted.
Create a new project in the PostHog dashboard.
Install the PostHog SDK in your app or website.
Send your first event to verify integration.
Explore dashboards and set up basic reports.
entry.whenToUse: Use PostHog to quickly add product analytics and event tracking to web or mobile apps without vendor lock-in.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced analytics at high scale or require enterprise-level support and SLAs.
entry.body: PostHog provides open-source product analytics with a generous free tier, making it easy to track user behavior and product usage for small teams and projects.

entry.title: SikkerAPI
entry.description: SikkerAPI provides a secure API gateway with rate limiting, authentication, and analytics for developers and teams.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes basic API gateway features with limited monthly requests.
caveats: No custom domain support on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free SikkerAPI account.
Register your API endpoint in the dashboard.
Configure rate limits and authentication settings.
Obtain your API key and update your application to route traffic through SikkerAPI.
Monitor usage and analytics in the dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use SikkerAPI when you need to secure, monitor, and limit access to your APIs without managing your own gateway infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require custom domains, advanced routing, or unlimited requests, or if you need on-premise deployment.
entry.body: SikkerAPI offers a simple, secure API gateway with built-in rate limiting, authentication, and analytics, ideal for small teams and indie developers.

entry.title: Virgil Security
entry.description: Virgil Security provides cryptographic tools and APIs for end-to-end encryption, authentication, and secure key management.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes basic cryptographic APIs and limited usage for key management and authentication.
limits: Up to 1,000 active users per month
caveats: Support is limited on free tier

useCases: User authentication with public key infrastructure
Secure key management for SaaS products
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Virgil Security account.
Create a new application in the Virgil dashboard.
Obtain your API credentials.
Integrate the Virgil SDK into your app using provided documentation.
Test encryption, decryption, and authentication flows.
entry.whenToUse: Use Virgil Security when you need to add end-to-end encryption or secure key management to your application quickly.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced cryptographic customization or need to self-host all security infrastructure.
entry.body: Virgil Security offers APIs and SDKs for developers to implement encryption, authentication, and key management, making it easier to secure user data and communications.
